title: Adds more docker py test
body:
Addes more docker py test
Optimize test to import images from cache
Rename test class and dir for python unittest framework

Signed-off-by: Sujil02 <sushah@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *sujil02* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

The README.md is pretty seriously wrong. Could you please update it to make it possible for someone to actually try running the tests? For instance:
* Indicate the working directory where someone needs to be cd'ed
* fix PYTHONPATH in all examples, to align with ^^
* add missing `-m unittest` to first example
* change directory and file paths (i.e. add `_test`)
* Indicate why sudo is necessary, or remove it
* indicate which packages need to be dnf-installed (for `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'docker'`)

...possibly more. Thanks.
LGTM, would be nice to get @edsantiago 's concerns addressed in at least a separate PR.  @sujil02 if you can not do so today, please create a new issue.

> The README.md is pretty seriously wrong. Could you please update it to make it possible for someone to actually try running the tests? For instance:
>

> * Indicate why sudo is necessary, or remove it
> * indicate which packages need to be dnf-installed (for `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'docker'`)

> ...possibly more. Thanks.

Thank you @edsantiago I will get in a separate pr addressing this.
